issue.title: [Bug]: Terminal scrolls to top after Claude output
issue.body:
### Current Behavior

When using Claude and arbitrary points, but always when Claude asks for input, the Wave terminal will scroll all the way to the top

### Expected Behavior

Don't scroll to the top :)

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Use wave
2. Run Claude
3. Ask claude to do something

### Wave Version

0.11.6

### Platform

macOS

### OS Version/Distribution

Tahoe 26.0

### Architecture

arm64

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Questionnaire

- [ ] I'm interested in fixing this myself but don't know where to start
- [ ] I would like to fix and I have a solution
- [ ] I don't have time to fix this right now, but maybe later

@sawka I came here searching for this as it's too much annoying and wanting me to move to some other terminal. I have recorded a video for you to see that within 2 mins of Claude working, it scrolled back to top like 3 times and then I had to come back down again.

issue.title: [Testing] Increase test coverage for security-critical paths
issue.body:
## Problem
Current test suite has 3 test files with 7 tests. For a security tool, this is insufficient. Key untested paths:

## Areas Needing Coverage
1. **Scoring engine** — Edge cases: zero confidence, boundary scores (6.99 vs 7.0), invalid weights
2. **Pattern matching** — BasePlugin.analyze() with various regex matches/misses
3. **HttpAdapter** — Retry behavior, rate limiting, timeout handling, error responses
4. **Config loader** — Invalid YAML, missing fields, env var overrides, profile merging
5. **Plugin discovery** — Empty directories, malformed plugins, duplicate IDs
6. **SARIF reporter** — Schema compliance, findings mapping

## Target
- Increase from 7 tests to ~30+
- Focus on security-critical scoring and detection paths first

## Priority
P1 — Directly impacts credibility when security teams evaluate the tool.

comment.body: The duplicate-ID test is 🔥 — exactly the kind of guard that prevents silent plugin shadowing. One suggestion: the test for malformed exports uses a loose regex Missing \meta.id\|prompts — it might be worth tightening this to match the exact error message thrown by PluginValidationError so it doesn't inadvertently pass on a refactored error string.

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
+import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
comment.body: The retry + timeout tests are particularly valuable here — these code paths are easy to miss manually. One gap: there's no test for the uthToken being sent as the Authorization header. Would be worth adding a test that asserts  etchMock was called with Authorization: Bearer <token> in the request headers.

Nice use of i.stubEnv + fterEach(() => vi.unstubAllEnvs()) — clean isolation between tests. One thing to add: a test covering MANTIS_TARGET_URL env override when no CLI url is provided would complete the parity with the auth token test.

Great addition of the  aseFinding() factory — this is exactly the right pattern to reduce boilerplate and keep tests readable. One small suggestion: consider moving it into a shared __fixtures__/finding.ts helper so it can be reused across other test files (e.g., if we add engine integration tests later).

issue.title: README.fr.md
issue.body:
# 🛡️ 90 Jours de Cybersécurité — Plan d'Études (Version Française)

Bienvenue dans le défi **90 Jours de Cybersécurité** !  
Ce plan autodidacte structuré vous accompagnera pendant 90 jours pour acquérir les bases solides de la cybersécurité, tout en pratiquant chaque jour.

---

## 📚 Table des matières

1. [Introduction](#introduction)
2. [Objectifs et Public cible](#objectifs-et-public-cible)
3. [Déroulement par jour](#déroulement-par-jour)
4. [Comment contribuer](#comment-contribuer)
5. [Contributeurs](#contributeurs)

---

## 📘 Introduction

Ce programme est conçu pour :
- Les débutants souhaitant entrer dans le domaine de la cybersécurité
- Les professionnels de l’IT en reconversion
- Les étudiants en informatique, télécom, réseaux
- Les passionnés autodidactes

Vous apprendrez des concepts clés tels que :
- Le réseau (Network+)
- La sécurité informatique (Security+)
- Linux et les scripts Shell
- Python pour l’automatisation de la sécurité
- Analyse de trafic réseau
- Git et le contrôle de version
- Stack ELK et analyse de logs
- Sécurité dans le cloud (GCP, AWS, Azure)
- Hacking éthique & Pentesting

Chaque jour propose des tutoriels, exercices, outils à tester ou vidéos à regarder.

---

## 🎯 Objectifs et Public cible

### 🎯 Objectifs

- Acquérir une base solide en cybersécurité
- Pratiquer au quotidien pendant 90 jours
- Se préparer aux certifications (CompTIA Network+ et Security+)
- Se familiariser avec des outils réels (Wireshark, Git, Suricata, ELK, etc.)
- Développer des automatismes avec Linux et Python
- Découvrir les plateformes de hacking éthique

### 👤 Public Cible

- Débutants sans prérequis avancé
- Étudiants en génie informatique, réseaux, sécurité
- Administrateurs ou techniciens réseaux
- Développeurs et DevOps curieux de la sécurité
- Passionnés de cybersécurité et autodidactes

---

## 🗓️ Déroulement par Jour (Résumé)

| Jours | Thème |
|-------|-------|
| 1–7   | Réseaux (Network+) |
| 8–14  | Sécurité (Security+) |
| 15–28 | Linux et Shell |
| 29–42 | Python pour la cybersécurité |
| 43–56 | Analyse de trafic (Wireshark, Suricata) |
| 57–63 | Git et versionning |
| 64–70 | Stack ELK (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) |
| 71–77 | Cloud (GCP, AWS, Azure) |
| 78–84 | Révision et renforcement |
| 85–90 | Hacking éthique (HackTheBox, VulnHub) |
| 91–92 | CV en cybersécurité |
| 93–95 | Candidatures et recherche d'emploi |

Pour les liens utiles, tutoriels et ressources détaillées : voir le fichier `LEARN.fr.md`.

---

## 🙌 Comment contribuer

Tu veux aider ce projet à grandir ? Voici quelques idées :

- 📝 Ajouter des tutoriels ou résumés de concepts
- 🧪 Proposer des quiz ou mini-exercices
- 📘 Traduire des pages en français
- 🧰 Partager des outils ou scripts utiles
- 💬 Corriger/améliorer le contenu existant
- 🎓 Proposer des cas réels ou études de sécurité

**Aucune expérience requise**, toute contribution est précieuse !  
→ Voir `CONTRIBUTING.md` pour les étapes de soumission sur GitHub.

---

## 🏆 Contributeurs

Merci à toutes celles et ceux qui rendent ce projet possible !  
Mention spéciale à :

- Farhan Ashraf  
- Wanderer479  
- Muhammad Mahad  
- André Oliveira  
- arbenp  
- paduh  
- Et vous bientôt ? 😎

---

📩 **Contact ou collaboration :**  
Tu souhaites rejoindre l’équipe francophone ou créer des contenus avec nous ? Écris-nous sur GitHub ou via les issues du projet.

---

**#90DaysOfCyberSecurity | Ensemble vers l’excellence en cybersécurité ! 🔐**

discussion.title: Website UX & Community Improvements – Making CNS PK More Contributor-Friendly
discussion.body:
## Context

As Cloud Native Security Pakistan grows, our website should evolve from being purely informational into a strong community entry point.

This issue is to open a structured discussion around:

- Improving overall user experience
- Making navigation clearer and simpler
- Reducing friction for first-time visitors
- Encouraging more open-source contributions
- Making the platform feel modern, clean, and community-driven

Let’s align and improve it together.

---

## 🎯 Goals

- Help newcomers instantly understand what CNS PK is
- Improve visibility of Join / Contribute / Attend Events
- Highlight events, chapters, and initiatives clearly
- Improve mobile responsiveness
- Improve performance (Lighthouse target: 90+)
- Make contribution pathways obvious and easy

---

## 🧠 Discussion Points

- What feels confusing right now?
- What should be removed?
- What is missing?
- How can we improve the first-time visitor experience?
- Should we add:
  - Member showcase?
  - Blog or resources section?
  - Event archive?
  - Contributor recognition?
  - Beginner “Start Here” page?

---

## 🛠 Suggested Improvements

- Cleaner hero section with strong CTA
- Better event visibility
- Simplified navigation
- Newsletter integration
- Dark mode support
- Stronger GitHub visibility
- Clear contribution guide

---

## 🤝 Collaboration

If you're interested in improving the website:

- Drop your suggestions below
- Share UI ideas (Figma / sketches / screenshots)
- Create small, focused PRs
- Keep changes modular and iterative

Goal: Build a strong digital front door for the Cloud Native Security community in Pakistan.

issue.title: feat: enable perfect 100/100 scores by removing efficiency penalty floor
issue.body:
# 🌙 feat(ramadan): Enable Perfect 100/100 Score by Reducing Efficiency Penalty  

## 🎯 Summary  
Removed the artificial ceiling on perfect submissions by reducing the minimum efficiency penalty from `6` → `0` for submissions ≤90 tokens. **For the first time, users can achieve a true 100/100 score** (previously capped at 97.6).  

---

## 📊 Penalty Tier Changes  
| Token Range | Before | After | Impact |  
|-------------|--------|-------|--------|  
| **≤90 tokens** | 6 | **0** | 🚀 **+2.4 pts** (17.6 → **20.0**) |  
| ≤140 tokens | 10 | 6 | 📈 +1.6 pts |  
| ≤220 tokens | 16 | 12 | 📈 +1.6 pts |  
| ≤320 tokens | 24 | 20 | 📈 +1.6 pts |  
| >320 tokens | 32 | 28 | 📈 +1.6 pts |  

---

## ✨ Key Improvements  
✅ **Perfect score achievable**: 60 (Quality) + 20 (Correctness) + **20 (Efficiency)** = **100/100**  
✅ **More intuitive scoring**: Truly optimal submissions (≤90 tokens) now receive *zero penalty*  
✅ **Preserves competitiveness**: Concise prompts still dominate leaderboard (≤90 tier unchanged relative to others)  
✅ **User-friendly**: Reduces frustration while maintaining engineering rigor  
✅ **Zero breaking changes**: API unchanged, no DB migration required  



## ⚠️ Important Note  
**Historical score comparisons are inconsistent** between pre/post-update submissions.  
➡️ *Recommendation*:  
- Add UI badge: `🏆 Scored under v2.1+ (perfect score achievable)`  
- Reset leaderboard for new Ramadan season  
- Archive pre-update scores with context label  

---

## 💡 Why This Change?  
| Before | After |  
|--------|-------|  
| ❌ "Why can't I get 100?" (user frustration) | ✅ Clear path to perfection |  
| ❌ Arbitrary 97.6 ceiling | ✅ Mathematically clean 100 max |  
| ❌ Penalized *optimal* submissions | ✅ Rewards true excellence |  
| ❌ Confusing scoring narrative | ✅ "Write concise prompts → earn full points" |  

---

## 🔒 Backwards Compatibility  
- ✅ Existing submissions retain original scores  
- ✅ API contract unchanged (`computeWeightedScore` signature identical)  
- ✅ No database schema changes  
- ✅ All existing tests pass + new coverage added  

---

## 🌟 Impact  
> "Finally achieved 100/100 on Day 1! The scoring feels fair now – my concise prompt was *actually* perfect."  
> – *Hypothetical delighted user* 😄  

This change transforms scoring from *"What's the least bad penalty?"* to *"How perfectly can you engineer this prompt?"* – aligning with the spirit of Ramadan Prompting Nights. 🌙✨  

**Perfect is now possible.**

issue.title: feat: allow float values for delay

issue.title: Compress Platform banners
issue.body:
<!--
Thank you 🙏 for your contribution to [Ultralytics](https://www.ultralytics.com/) 🚀! Your effort in enhancing our repositories is greatly appreciated. To streamline the process and assist us in integrating your Pull Request (PR) effectively, please follow these steps:

1. Check for Existing Contributions: Before submitting, kindly explore existing PRs to ensure your contribution is unique and complementary.
2. Link Related Issues: If your PR addresses an open issue, please link it in your submission. This helps us better understand the context and impact of your contribution.
3. Elaborate Your Changes: Clearly articulate the purpose of your PR. Whether it's a bug fix or a new feature, a detailed description aids in a smoother integration process.
4. Ultralytics Contributor License Agreement (CLA): To uphold the quality and integrity of our project, we require all contributors to sign the CLA. Please confirm your agreement by commenting below:

    I have read the CLA Document and I sign the CLA

For more detailed guidance and best practices on contributing, refer to our ✅ [Contributing Guide](https://docs.ultralytics.com/help/contributing/). Your adherence to these guidelines ensures a faster and more effective review process.
-->

## 🛠️ PR Summary

<sub>Made with ❤️ by [Ultralytics Actions](https://www.ultralytics.com/actions)</sub>

### 🌟 Summary
Compressed Ultralytics Platform banner image assets to reduce file size while preserving their existing purpose and usage. 🖼️⚡

### 📊 Key Changes
- Optimized banner image assets for the `yolov8` directory.
- Updated `banner-vertical-yolov8.png` with a compressed version.
- Updated `banner-yolov8.png` with a compressed version.
- No functional code changes; this PR focuses on asset size optimization only.

### 🎯 Purpose & Impact
- Reduces repository asset weight, which can improve download and load efficiency. 🚀
- Helps streamline storage and bandwidth usage for banner assets.
- Maintains existing visuals while improving asset delivery performance for users and contributors.
<details><summary>📋 Skipped 2 files (lock files, generated, images, etc.)</summary>

- `yolov8/banner-vertical-yolov8.png`
- `yolov8/banner-yolov8.png`
</details>
